items.title: Gunman who killed at least 6, took hostages in Ukraine supermarket shot dead by police
items.summary_auto: A gunman who killed at least six people in the streets of Kyiv, Ukraine, Saturday before taking hostages and barricading himself in a nearby supermarket has been shot dead by police, Ukrainian officials said.

items.title: Here's the 'experiment' NDP Leader Avi Lewis is trying to run in Canadian politics
items.summary_auto: This week, Lewis made the rounds on Parliament Hill for the first time since becoming NDP leader. CBC's The House gave him a tour of West Block — the home of the House of Commons — as he detailed what his political "experiment" will look like.

items.title: B.C. bull rider ranked No. 1 in the world
items.summary_auto: Jake Gardner is at the top of his game, coming off a big championship win at RodeoHouston that kicked him to the top of the Professional Rodeo Cowboys Association standings for bull riding.

items.title: Pope Leo XIV says 'not in my interest at all' to debate Donald Trump
items.summary_auto: Pope Leo XIV says he won't debate U.S. President Donald Trump and will continue to preach peace.

items.title: Massive gas flare over Kitimat a beacon of LNG growth, and for some a source of worry
items.summary_auto: Flaring gas towering high over Kitimat is a regular sight as the  LNG Canada  export terminal — considered a nation-building project — comes online, but some residents are concerned about possible health impacts.

items.title: Iran reimposes Strait of Hormuz restrictions as tensions with U.S. continue
items.summary_auto: The duelling blockades in the Strait of Hormuz lurched into uncharted waters on Saturday as the United States pressed ahead with its campaign to choke off Iranian ports and Iran reversed an initial move to reopen the waterway.

items.title: Kelowna RCMP's new security camera network linked to businesses must be monitored: privacy watchdog
items.summary_auto: Kelowna businesses are being invited to register their security cameras so that police have a straightforward way of requesting footage. But the head of a B.C. privacy watch group says he's concerned about the impact to personal privacy.

items.title: Summer gas is here. What does that mean for sky-high prices?
items.summary_auto: As a gas tax break comes down the pipe from Ottawa, the summer blend of gas means prices could still move in the wrong direction for already-stretched motorists.

items.title: 'He's going to be stuck': Guelph, Ont., family struggles to afford speech therapy for 4-year-old son
items.summary_auto: A Guelph, Ont., family says their four-year-old son requires intensive speech therapy for his childhood apraxia of speech and OHIP doesn't fully cover the sessions he needs to make progress. They've planned a fundraiser for Saturday afternoon to help them cover costs and also raise awareness of the condition.

items.title: How 1 bad butter tart sent this man on a mission to protect the Canadian treat
items.summary_auto: John Meissner has loved butter tarts since he was a little boy living at a farm in Guelph, Ont. But it wasn't until he had an offensively bad butter tart while judging at a festival in February that he decided he needed to do something to conserve the original recipe of the dessert.

items.title: Health Canada warned against buying peptides online. CBC News easily bought some
items.summary_auto: Companies are selling peptides on the grey market, despite warnings from Health Canada.

items.title: Fear of reprisals, isolation, anxiety: Report documents mental health concerns at CSIS
items.summary_auto: Canadian Security Intelligence Service employees who came forward as part of an external study investigating mental health at the spy agency described a sometimes isolating and bleak environment "laden with stigma."

items.title: Electric ferries are breaking records — and quietly joining Canada's fleet
items.summary_auto: Electric ferries around the world are getting bigger and travelling longer distances than ever. They're also quietly arriving at quays across Canada. Here's where they're at across the country.

items.title: Palestinian football officials worry Canada won't approve visas in time for FIFA meeting
items.summary_auto: Media reports published this week suggested Canada had blocked members of the Palestinian Football Association from entering the country ahead of the FIFA Congress in Vancouver later this month. However, it appears that Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada (IRCC) has yet to issue final visa decisions.

items.title: Toronto renters gather this weekend to launch first citywide tenants union
items.summary_auto: Organizers say the new union will unite tenants across Toronto to strengthen collective action on rent, housing conditions, and affordability, while landlords and housing providers also weigh in on the tensions in the rental market.

items.title: The time has come to jump on the Toronto Raptors bandwagon
items.summary_auto: The NBA's first round of playoffs begin Saturday, and for the first time in four years, the Toronto Raptors will be in the fight. And while the pundits may not be calling for Toronto to come out on top,  don't try telling star Scottie Barnes the Raptors are the underdogs.

items.title: Teen's death prompts push to ban energy drink sales to Quebec youth under 16
items.summary_auto: The family of a Quebec teen who died after mixing energy drinks and medication is joined by school groups in calling for a ban on energy drink sales to youth under 16, citing health risks and growing support for tighter rules.

items.title: Reopening the Strait of Hormuz is easier said than done. Here's why
items.summary_auto: Not for the first time in the past seven weeks of war and tenuous ceasefire, the details of developments concerning the Strait of Hormuz have been sparse, creating unease and anxiety among the shipping companies and crews whose tankers and containers slowly circle the waters either side of the critical waterway.

items.title: U.S. Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick takes a swipe at Canada's trade strategy ahead of review
items.summary_auto: U.S. Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick on Friday criticized the current iteration of North America's free trade agreement as a bad deal, taking a swipe at Canada's trade strategy ahead of upcoming negotiations.

items.title: Community shocked after judge dismisses charges against driver who killed 3 people
items.summary_auto: Hundreds of people outside a courtroom expressed shock and dismay Friday after an Ontario judge dismissed dangerous driving causing death charges against a driver who killed three members of a family in a Markham, Ont., crash.
